title: The Red Queen
subtitle: Sex and the Evolution of Human Nature
author: Matt Ridley
themes: The Red Queen hypothesis — organisms must keep evolving just to stay in the same place relative to their evolving competitors and parasites
Sex exists because it provides genetic variation to outpace parasites (the Red Queen dynamic)
Sexual selection — mate choice — is a powerful evolutionary force that shapes both bodies and minds
Human sexuality, from courtship to marriage, reflects evolutionary strategies shaped by sexual selection
The human mind is as much a product of sexual selection as natural selection
excerpt: The Red Queen said, 'Now, here, you see, it takes all the running you can do to keep in the same place.' The evolutionary version: every organism is running as fast as it can, not to get ahead, but to stay in the same place relative to its ever-evolving competitors, predators, and parasites. Sex is the ultimate Red Queen strategy — a way to keep shuffling the genetic deck so that parasites cannot predict the next hand.
